settings.image_alt: Oiled solid wood table with a dark ceramic bowl
settings.title: Oil Finish
settings.text: Nourish every 6–12 months with natural oil to maintain depth and protection.

settings.image_alt: Matte walnut cabinet corner with soft grain detail
settings.title: Matte Finish
settings.text: Clean gently and avoid waxes. Use soft cloths and mild, natural cleaners.

settings.image_alt: Live-edge natural wood slab with visible grain and a book
settings.title: Natural Grain
settings.text: Embrace unique markings and variations—part of the wood's natural story.

settings.question: Can I place hot items directly on the wood surface?
settings.answer: We recommend using a trivet or heat pad. Extreme heat can damage the finish or cause cracks over time.

settings.question: How often should I oil my furniture?
settings.answer: For oil-finished pieces, reapply a thin coat of natural oil every 6 to 12 months, or whenever the surface begins to look dry.

settings.question: Why does my wood change color over time?
settings.answer: Natural wood develops a warm patina as it responds to light and air. This gentle shift in tone is a normal, cherished part of solid wood aging.

settings.question: What should I avoid using to clean my furniture?
settings.answer: Avoid abrasive pads, silicone sprays, and harsh chemical cleaners. Use a soft cloth with water or a mild, natural cleaner instead.
